verified.notes: Indiana PathWays for Aging is NOT a PACE equivalent—it is a separate Medicaid managed long-term services and supports (MLTSS) program. PACE is a distinct program for ages 55+ in specific service areas. PathWays replaced the Aged & Disabled Waiver effective July 1, 2024. Income limit of $2,982/month is for 2026 individual applicants; asset limits were not found in provided sources. Phone number is the official Indiana PathWays for Aging Helpline (877-PATHWAY-4).

programs.programName: Indiana SHIP (State Health Insurance Assistance Program)

verified.notes: Indiana SHIP is a counseling program for Medicare beneficiaries with no stated age, income, or asset eligibility requirements in available sources. The program provides free, impartial information and assistance to all Medicare consumers regardless of financial status. Phone number confirmed from official IN.gov SHIP website and multiple authoritative sources.

programs.notes: Indiana SHIP is a counseling program for Medicare beneficiaries with no stated age, income, or asset eligibility requirements in available sources. The program provides free, impartial information and assistance to all Medicare consumers regardless of financial status. Phone number confirmed from official IN.gov SHIP website and multiple authoritative sources.

verified.notes: Meals on Wheels in Indiana operates as multiple independent local programs (Central Indiana/Marion County, Hamilton County, Hancock County, Muncie, Northwest Indiana). No single statewide program exists. Phone number provided is for Meals on Wheels of Central Indiana, the largest program serving Marion County since 1971. Search results do not contain specific age, income, or asset eligibility thresholds for any Indiana program. Hamilton County program states service is available to 'anyone who is physically or mentally unable to prepare a meal for themselves regardless of age or income,' suggesting no formal eligibility limits, but this requires direct contact to confirm current policy.
